compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Context
rebar records a rich, durable, event-sourced history — ticket events on the `tickets` branch (compaction renames folded events to committed `.retired` files, so the raw history survives synced), gate sidecars (`REVIEW_RESULT`, `COMPLETION_VERDICT`), the two-file module-size ratchet, and git — but there is no aggregation layer and no way to ask "how is the agent-driven dev loop trending over time?". Several agent-lifecycle signals are blindspots today (env/integration-diagnosis time, "which change caused a bug", replanning/escalation classification), and code-health movement (coverage/complexity/clone) is computed ephemerally in CI and never persisted. This epic delivers a single dynamic date-range `rebar metrics` command surfacing every available metric across three lenses — is the agent loop working, is the codebase decaying, are the gates worth it — plus the minimal passive instrumentation to close the blindspots without any new agent-facing manual command.

**"Tier-C blindspots" (defined):** the three agent-lifecycle signal categories that leave no durable trace in the event store today and so cannot be derived structurally — (a) env/integration-diagnosis intervals (an infra failure starts a diagnosis, its next clean gate ends it), (b) bug provenance ("which prior change/ticket caused this bug"), and (c) bug outcome classification (regression vs plan-defect vs flaky vs …). Each is closed by a passive emission stamped by trustworthy code, never by agent self-report.

## Success Criteria
- `rebar metrics --since <date> --until <date>` emits a machine-readable artifact spanning agent-process, gate-economics, and code-health metrics, with real date-range trends for every metric that has a complete history.
- The command is built once against a complete metric registry: metrics whose signals have not yet accrued report a structured `unavailable` state and light up automatically as data arrives, with no future code change.
- Tier-C blindspots (defined above) are captured passively: env/integration-diagnosis via an `ERROR` outcome on the existing gate sidecars; bug provenance via a new `caused_by` link relation (best-effort auto-derived from blame, plus an explicit override param); bug classification via a persisted close `--class` enum replacing today's validated-then-discarded free-text reason.
- **Bug-close friction stays flat (operationalized):** `rebar transition <bug> in_progress closed` requires exactly one bug-specific argument both before and after this epic — today a REQUIRED free-text `--reason`, afterward a REQUIRED bounded `--class` enum. The required-argument count for a bug close does not increase from 1 to 2; verified by diffing `rebar transition --help` against the pre-epic baseline (the change swaps one required arg for another, adds none). No non-bug transition gains a new required argument.
- Historical data is backfilled wherever a durable source exists; **every** backfilled/classified value is labeled `source=backfill_classified` and `confidence=classified` (the single backfill vocabulary — there is no `backfill_structural`), and `is_authoritative()` returns False for it so it is never aggregated into an authoritative structural series.

## Approach
Derive-structurally over the durable event store (including `.retired` events) for everything with a clean event signature; add the smallest possible passive emission only where a signal genuinely leaves no trace, always stamped by trustworthy code (gate error handlers, git blame) rather than agent self-report — the layered-hybrid design that dev-analytics (DevLake, Swarmia) and agent-observability practice converge on, and that fits rebar's event-sourced substrate. We reuse existing surfaces (gate sidecars, the `transition` command, the link-relation set) rather than introduce a new event type, keeping every change additive and back-compatible (old records stay valid; readers tolerate absence). The command and its structural sources are harness- and target-project-agnostic (a rebar CLI over the portable event store); harness/CI-specific backfills are isolated optional adapters the core command never depends on.

**Blame-derivation is best-effort and non-authoritative.** `caused_by` auto-derivation blames the **pre-fix** state — the parent of the fixing commit (`<fix>~1`) — over the bug's recorded `file_impact`, never the post-fix HEAD (which would attribute the fixing commit itself). When a bug has no `file_impact`, spans multiple files ambiguously, or blame cannot resolve a single commit, auto-derivation is **skipped** (no wrong edge is written); the explicit `--caused-by <id>` override always wins. Auto-derived `caused_by` edges feed only `classified`-confidence provenance metrics and are never treated as authoritative — accuracy is a labeled heuristic, not a guarantee.

**Auto-link vs the closed-source guard (555e ordering).** The auto-link runs *after* `txn.transition_core` commits the STATUS event, so the bug (the link SOURCE) is already `closed` when blame runs; `graph._links.add_dependency` rejects links from a closed source (`_links.py:242-247`). So 555e writes the edge via `graph._links._write_link_event(bug, culprit, "caused_by", ...)` **directly**, bypassing the closed-source and cycle guards — which add nothing for a non-blocking, non-cycle-inducing relation on a closed source. Without this, every auto-derived edge would be silently swallowed and the feature would be a dead letter.

**Snapshot store path.** The going-forward snapshot series (3275) is committed to `.rebar/metrics-snapshots.ndjson` — a **git-tracked** path in the rebar store — NOT under `reports/`, which is gitignored (`.gitignore:74`) and would silently drop every snapshot.

**Back-out / expand-contract for the bug-close CLI change (ed13):** the `--class` enum replaces the required `--reason` on the *bug-close* path only; old STATUS records without `close_class` still reduce cleanly (readers tolerate absence), and back-out is simply ceasing to require/write the field. Because `--class` becomes required on bug close, its error message must enumerate the allowed values so any existing caller that omitted it fails loudly with an actionable message rather than silently.

## Decomposition
Three tracks; each child is a single concern (detail lives in the children):
- Command: metric-registry + unavailable/label contract; event-derivation readers; git-derivation readers; gate-sidecar reader + `ERROR` path; going-forward snapshot writer/reader; the `rebar metrics` CLI.
- Instrumentation: the `caused_by` link relation (additive); the bug close `--class` enum (persisted, replacing the discarded reason); blame-based `caused_by` auto-derivation + `--caused-by` param + bug-template advisory.
- Backfill (one-time, labeled): `caused_by` over existing bugs; historical bug `--class` from structural signals; complexity/clone snapshots at sampled commits; local Claude-Code-transcript mining (optional adapter); GitHub-Actions coverage + CI-health harvest (optional adapter).

**Cross-ticket coordination (shared-file ordering the dep graph does not encode):**
- `ed13` (bug close `--class`) and `555e` (`--caused-by` + blame) both edit `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py` and `transition_close.py`; land **ed13 before 555e** and stack 555e on top so the close-guard restructure exists first. Any `555e`/`2f8e` test that closes a bug MUST pass `--class` (it is required after ed13).
- `0b96` (`caused_by` relation) and `9a5a` (metrics command) both touch `src/rebar/_cli/help/overview.txt` and regenerate `tests/golden/cli_help/__overview_help.out`; 0b96 lands first (Layer 0), and 9a5a re-regenerates the golden on top.
- Layer-1 reader children (`18e6`, `3c07`, `7931`) each register into `src/rebar/metrics/registry.py`; land them sequentially up the stack (or via an append-only registration seam) so registry edits do not collide.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All child tickets are closed (epic rollup), verified by `rebar list --epic <this> --status open` returning none.
- [ ] `rebar metrics --since 2026-01-01 --until 2026-07-01 --output json` exits 0 and emits JSON where every registered metric key is present with either a value or `{"unavailable":{"reason","accruing_since"}}` — an integration test asserts via `jq -e` that no registered metric key is absent (the capstone 9a5a AC asserts this exact no-missing-key check, not merely a non-empty map).
- [ ] Every metric entry carries `source` and `confidence`, and a `source=backfill_classified` value is never aggregated into an authoritative structural series — a unit test asserts `is_authoritative('backfill_classified')` is False and the segregation holds.
- [ ] Bug-close friction stays flat: the required free-text reason is replaced (not supplemented) by a bounded `--class` enum persisted on the STATUS event, verified by `rebar show <closed-bug> --output json | jq -e '.close_class'`, AND a `rebar transition --help` diff shows no net increase in required bug-close arguments.
- [ ] Backfill is validated: at least one backfilled `caused_by`/`close_class`/snapshot value exists carrying `source=backfill_classified` + `confidence=classified`, asserted by a test over the backfill output.
compiled_state.title: Passive metrics + agent-lifecycle instrumentation (rebar metrics command)
